svg.text: Figure 2. The feature-first thesis for tabular enterprise data
For most enterprise decisions, marginal return is higher from features and validation than from model complexity.
High marginal return: spend scale here
Feature engineering: calendar and event windows, exogenous context (CPI,
Honest validation: out-of-period testing, drift monitoring, calibration.
Lower marginal return: do not over-invest
Model complexity for its own sake: deep networks where well-tuned tree
ensembles already win on tabular data, at far higher tuning and opacity cost.
Rational default model for tabular enterprise data
Tree ensembles (random forests, Extremely Randomized Trees, gradient boosting such as XGBoost):
strong accuracy, low tuning cost, and native feature-importance for decision support and audit.
Use deep models only when they beat a well-validated tree-ensemble baseline by enough to justify cost and opacity.
